_comment: Macro-Terrain: Ancient God Finger Mountain. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: Ancient God Finger Mountain
scale: 200 li long, 30 li wide, 5 li tall — one finger of a being whose body spanned a continent.
canonical_event: Tu Si's Ancient God body fell here; Wang Lin inherited Tu Si's god-knowledge here.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A mountain range of unusual grey stone, 200 li long, peaks oddly knuckle-shaped.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The stone has a faint bone-like grain; cold to the touch regardless of season.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The 'stone' is clearly petrified bone; marrow-channels visible in cross-sections.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is an Ancient God's finger. The peaks are knuckle-joints; the valleys are tendon-grooves.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The finger still holds residual god-essence; marrow-channels pulse faintly with ancient qi.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The entire finger is a relic of Tu Si's Ancient God body; god-essence permeates every grain.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
